text: Board put consolidation back on the agenda. Sage Creek and Pronghorn Valley both on the chopping block.
Knew it was coming. They want the numbers to do the talking.
Then we bring the families. Numbers cut both ways.
I can pull the busing costs they keep leaving out. Hour each way for the little ones.
Eighty five families at Sage Creek. We need most of them in that room.
I'll start the phone tree. Got about thirty numbers already.
Good. Tell them plain: show up or the school's gone.
Need three people for the sign-in table at the December meeting. Who's in.
Me.
Count me too. I'll bring coffee.
I can run the third chair.
Meeting went long. Board tabled the vote to February. We bought time, not a win.
Got a quote on 200 signs. Keep Our Schools Open. About a dollar forty each.
Order them. I'll cover the deposit and we settle up after.
Pickup at my place once they're in. I'm on the Cody road, easy to find.
Signs are in. Come get a stack and put them where folks drive.
The November vote is the one. Three on the board are leaning close. Caldwell, talk to them.
Already on it. Coffee with two of them Thursday.
Phone tree's done twice over. People are fired up about the bus times.
Vote delayed again. Superintendent wants another study. We hold the line.
Thanks to everyone who showed. Room was full. That's what they remember.
Quiet stretch over the spring calving. Back at it soon. Keep the signs up.
Board reconvenes this month. The study landed and it's thin. Read it before the meeting.
Sage Creek family meeting set. Getting the room and the word out now.
Here is my read on the December 9 meeting. Three of us give testimony, we stay under two minutes each, and we hammer the busing cost. Nobody rambles.
Ran the mileage today. Closing Sage Creek pushes forty-one kids onto a bus route that runs out past the Southfork turnoff, about twenty-two miles one way.
That is roughly an extra hour and ten minutes on the bus each morning for the little ones. Try selling that to a kindergarten parent.
Superintendent Morrison is claiming the consolidation saves two hundred ten thousand a year. I asked for the line-item breakdown and got a one-page summary. That is it.
Sage Creek parents, if we lose this building our kids ride the bus before sunrise half the year. Please come to the December 9 meeting.
I am building the Sage Creek ride list. If you need a seat in a car to the district office, reply here with how many.
The Halversons on County Road 6 can take four kids and two adults. We have room in the truck.
Volunteer sign up for December 9. I need two people at the sign-in table, one on coffee, and two to hand out our fact sheet at the door.
Put me on coffee. I already told Charles I am hauling the urn from the Grange.
I will take the sign-in table. I know most faces so I can get folks registered for comment fast.
I will do the door with the fact sheets. Give me a stack of a hundred, we will need them.
One page. After we asked three times. Maria, can you rebuild their math from the transportation budget we already have?
Yard sign update. The print shop in Powell quoted us two dollars forty a sign for a run of two hundred. That is the best price yet.
Two forty is fair. Let us order two hundred to start. Keep Our Schools Open, green on white, big letters folks can read from the road.
Ordered. They will be ready for pickup Friday afternoon in Powell. I can grab them with the stock trailer.
I will ride along to Powell and help load. Two hundred signs is more than one man wants to stack.
Working on it. If they are counting a full teacher retirement as savings, that money walks out the door whether we consolidate or not.
Sage Creek has eighty-five families this year, not the sixty they keep quoting in the paper. Where are they getting sixty?
Putting my hand up for two adults needing a ride. My husband is shorthanded on the place this year and cannot leave the place.
They are counting head count on one October day and ignoring the mid-year moves. Ranch families register late. I have the real roster.
Great, that leaves one more for sign-in and one more for fact sheets. Who is in?

Next Park County School Board meeting is set for December 9 at 6pm in the district office on Sheridan Ave. If we want the consolidation vote tabled, we need bodies in those seats. Mark your calendars.
As a teacher I will say it plain, you cannot run a combined K-5 in the Pronghorn Valley building without portables. It is already tight.
Got you down, Peggy. Dale, can you grab Peggy on your way in off Road 6?
Good point. Add the cost of portable classrooms to our counter-numbers. That eats their savings fast.
Let us assign the testimony. I take busing and safety. Maria takes the enrollment count, politely. Susan takes classroom capacity. Agreed?
Agreed. I will keep it to the building, not personalities.
I would not call it fraud out loud, Charles. Call it an accounting error and let them correct it in public.
Easy. I go right past their gate. Peggy, be ready at 5:20.
For Sage Creek families, our real enrollment is eighty-five households, not the sixty the district keeps saying. If your family is not on my list, message me.
I taught second grade at Sage Creek for eleven years. That building is the heart of the west end. Tell the board what it means to you.
I can take a fact sheet spot until my testimony, then hand my stack to someone.
I will sit sign-in with Janet for the first half, then I need to get to my kids.
Perfect, table is fully staffed. I will print the fact sheets Saturday at the copy shop on Sheridan.
Send me the fact sheet draft first, I want the busing numbers right before a hundred copies go out with a typo.
Fair. Softer words, same hook.
Appreciate it Dale. We can stage them in my barn and folks can pick up from there.
Where do people grab signs? A lot of parents cannot get out to Carl's place off the highway.
Fair. I will drop fifty at the co-op in town and fifty at the feed store so folks in Cody can pick up easy.
Can I get five for the Sage Creek families on my road? I will hand them out at pickup Monday.
Phone tree update, I have twenty-two families confirmed for the 9th and nine more maybes. Aiming for forty in the room.
I can bring the folding table and the sign-in clipboard. Want me there by 5:15 to set up?
My twins started there this fall. The idea of a fifty minute bus ride for six year olds keeps me up at night.
Yes Tom, 5:15 is perfect. Coffee too if you can swing it, folks testify better warmed up.
Good catch. Draft coming to you tonight, Maria. Do not let me embarrass us with bad math.
Reminder that public comment sign-up closes at 5:45 that night, so get there early. I will have the sheet by the door.
I will haul the coffee urn from the Grange hall. Made plenty of bad coffee on that thing, but it works.
Turnout check, I have nineteen Sage Creek families saying yes to the 9th. If half the parents show, that room is ours.
Morrison's office just moved the finance presentation to the front of the agenda. Smart of them, they want it done before the room fills up.
Then we get there for the start, not the public comment. Push the arrival time up to 5:45 sharp everybody.
Did anyone see the letter in the Cody Enterprise defending the closure? Signed by someone on the budget committee. We need a response by Thursday deadline.
I will draft a rebuttal tonight. Charles and Barbara, will you sign it with me so it reads as the coalition and not just a teacher griping?
My grandkids went through Sage Creek. I am no parent anymore but I will stand up and say my piece too.
Thank you Carl. The board needs to hear from the ranch families who built this school, not just the young parents.
Reminder, the bus route they are proposing runs out past the Southfork turnoff. That is twenty-two miles for the far families.
We also need callers for the phone tree this week. Fifteen minutes and a call list, that is the whole ask.
I will take a call list. Give me the Sage Creek west end families, I know most of them.
I will call the ranch folks along the South Fork. They do not answer for strangers but they will answer for me.
After the meeting we need cleanup, two people to fold tables and haul the urn back. I will stay.
Count me in. Keep it to three hundred words, the Enterprise cuts anything longer.
Five is yours Peggy. Grab them from my barn or catch me at the feed store Saturday morning.
Placement matters. Get them on the county road where the buses run, that is where the board members drive.
Good thinking Tom. Put a cluster right at the Sage Creek turnoff and along Road 6 where traffic slows.
I staked six along Road 6 this evening. They pop against the hay fields, you cannot miss them.
I will sign. Lead with the busing miles, not the emotion. The miles are undeniable.
Sending you both the mileage table and the corrected enrollment so the letter has hard figures in it.
If you cannot make the meeting, you can still sign the letter. I will bring the clipboard to church Sunday and to the co-op Monday.
As a Sage Creek mom I can give the two minute human side if you want it. My twins would be on that Southfork route.
I will stay for cleanup too. Many hands, we are out in ten minutes.
I posted the updated enrollment and busing spreadsheet in the consolidation channel. Short version, the numbers do not back up the district's savings claim.
Peggy, yes. Save you for last, right before the vote. A parent voice lands harder than my spreadsheets.
Anybody able to watch little ones during the meeting so parents can focus? Susan mentioned the library has a room.
I will make sure Peggy has a ride and a seat up front. Nobody testifies from the back row.
Heads up, one board member told me privately he is leaning our way if we show real turnout. Turnout is the whole game now.
That is the best news all week. Fill the room and we give him cover to vote no.
Setback, the district put out a flyer saying combined schools mean more electives. Parents are asking me about it. How do we answer?
The library will let us use the back room from 5:30 to 8. I can line up two sitters if we know a headcount.
Sign me up for childcare drop off, that solves my whole problem for the night. Thank you Susan.
Great, that already freed up three more Sage Creek parents to come. Keep them coming.
Thank you all for stepping up. This table is the first thing folks see. A warm welcome brings them back next time.
One more task, someone to take a headcount photo of the crowd for the letter to the Enterprise. A full room is our best argument.
I will get the photo from the back before public comment starts. Wide shot, whole room.
Reminder to everyone working the table, wear the green Keep Our Schools Open button so folks know who to ask.
Ask them who teaches those electives. We have no staff budgeted for them. It is a promise with no money behind it.
We are already low in town. The co-op stack is half gone in two days. Can we reorder another hundred?
Calling Powell tomorrow for another hundred. Demand is a good problem to have.
